sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Kendrick Traction Splint (KTS) is a compact, lightweight traction splint used for isolated midshaft femur fractures. Its compact folding design makes it suitable for confined-space rescue and air medical transport.

sections.title: Application
sections.content:
1. Provide analgesia

2. Unfold and extend KTS to appropriate length (measured against uninjured leg)

3. Position ischial pad at ischial tuberosity; secure proximal strap

4. Apply ankle strap around foot/ankle

5. Connect traction cord to ankle strap

6. Apply gentle, steady traction by tightening ratchet or winch mechanism until pain is reduced

7. Secure support straps along thigh

8. Reassess neurovascular status

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Same indications and contraindications as Fernotrac
- Preferred in confined spaces and air transport due to compact design
- Reassess traction during patient movement
